allies.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory gives false-precision-avoidance the core constraint that makes unnecessary detail actively harmful rather than merely inconvenient.
allies.source_quote: FPA directly supports this model by acknowledging that working memory is limited.

allies.rationale_text: Simplification helps false-precision-avoidance compress the landscape into a usable map without carrying every decorative branch forward.
allies.source_quote: acting like maps that reveal key information while ignoring nonessential details

allies.rationale_text: Prioritization keeps false-precision-avoidance focused on the detail that changes action while dropping precision that does not change the decision.
allies.source_quote: The model is essential during the problem disaggregation and prioritization phase of problem-solving.

antagonists.rationale_text: Anchoring makes false-precision-avoidance necessary because one precise number can dominate judgment long after it stops being the right guide.
antagonists.source_quote: This cognitive bias occurs when people focus too much on a single, specific piece of information (the anchor).

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias turns detail into camouflage by collecting exhaustive support for the preferred conclusion instead of clarifying what actually matters.
antagonists.source_quote: Confirmation bias drives people to process information to get the conclusion they want.

structured_tensions.tension_text: False-precision-avoidance conflicts with base-rates when rounding away precision discards meaningful base-rate variation that would change the decision.
structured_tensions.source_quote: false-precision-avoidance conflicts with base-rates (base rates) when rounding away precision discards meaningful base-rate variation that would change the decision.

structured_tensions.tension_text: False-precision-avoidance conflicts with bayesian when avoidance of precise probability estimates prevents the quantitative prior-updating that Bayesian reasoning requires.
structured_tensions.source_quote: false-precision-avoidance conflicts with bayesian (Bayesian reasoning) when avoidance of precise probability estimates prevents the quantitative prior-updating that Bayesian reasoning requires.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strong on bounded approximation, cognitive-load relief, and anti-false-certainty pressure, so the file preserves that compact surface without forcing extra tensions.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor simplification relations beyond the strongest current-vocabulary links around cognitive load, prioritization, and anti-anchor pressure.
open_questions: Decision-trees pressure is source-real in the broader problem-solving framing, but the retained set favors cleaner direct current-vocabulary wording.
